title: Update octo-protocol to re-export Sender from hstry-core instead of defining its own
description:
## Context

Depends on: trx-7fh3 (Sender type in hstry-core)
Repo: octo (not hstry) -- but tracked here as a dependency note

Currently octo-protocol defines its own Sender struct. Once hstry-core has the canonical Sender type, octo-protocol should re-export it (like it does with Part) instead of having a duplicate definition.

## What to do (in octo repo)

1. Remove Sender/SenderType from octo-protocol/src/messages.rs
2. Add to octo-protocol lib.rs:
   pub use hstry_core::parts::{Sender, SenderType};  // or wherever it lives in hstry-core
3. Update octo-protocol Message to use the re-exported type
4. cargo check, cargo test

## Acceptance

- octo-protocol has no duplicate Sender definition
- Re-exports from hstry-core
- All tests pass
close_reason: hstry-core Sender type is now available in crates/hstry-core/src/parts.rs. Created octo-z5dx in octo repo to track the octo-protocol update.

title: Document sync parallelism and performance changes
close_reason: Documented sync parallelism in README

title: Adjust clippy print rules for CLI crates
close_reason: Done

title: Add quickstart command for auto scan+ingest

title: Add Hermes Agent adapter for hstry
description:
## Overview
Add support for syncing Hermes Agent (https://github.com/nousresearch/hermes-agent) conversations into hstry.

## Hermes Agent Overview
- Self-improving AI agent with 133k+ stars
- Uses Honcho for memory/user modeling
- Stores sessions in `~/.hermes/sessions/`
- Sessions are JSON files (not JSONL)

## Session Storage Format
Location: `~/.hermes/sessions/session_<timestamp>_<hash>.json`

Example structure:
```json
{
  "session_id": "20260417_083824_229f7c2c",
  "model": "qwen3.6-35b",
  "base_url": "http://zgx:8000/v1",
  "platform": null,
  "session_start": "2026-04-18T04:53:25.274422",
  "last_updated": "2026-04-18T04:53:41.105164",
  "system_prompt": "# Hermes Agent Persona\n\n...",
  "tools": [...]
}
```

## Implementation Notes

### Default Paths
- `~/.hermes/sessions/`
- Also check `~/.hermes/sessions/sessions.json` for session metadata

### Title Extraction
Similar to Codex adapter - the system_prompt contains AGENTS.md content that will pollute titles. Need to:
1. Skip system prompt for title extraction
2. Extract first real user message
3. Use session_id + project/cwd if available

### Challenges
- Full system prompt can be very large (like we saw - contains full AGENTS.md)
- Need to identify where messages start in the session file
- May need to look at Hermes state.db or additional files for complete message history

### Adapter Template
Use `adapters/codex/adapter.ts` as a reference since Hermes has similar session structure (JSON with metadata + tools + messages).

## Files to Create
- `adapters/hermes/adapter.ts`

## Testing
- Use Tommy's existing sessions at `~/.hermes/sessions/`
- Test with session files from April 2026
close_reason: Added adapters/hermes/adapter.ts. Parses ~/.hermes/sessions/session_*.json (canonical full transcript), maps roles, captures tool_calls/reasoning, derives provider from base_url, uses FRUM for title.

title: Add Aider adapter
Adapter protocol: add optional conversation version/message_count fields for import/export parity
description:
Now that conversations.version/message_count are canonical in hstry, adapter protocol structs should expose these as OPTIONAL fields for forward-compatible import/export flows.

Why
- Current hstry-runtime ParsedConversation/ExportConversation omit version/message_count entirely.
- External adapters can ignore them, but hstry-originating adapters/export tools lose metadata parity and cannot round-trip deterministic sync hints.

Scope
1) Extend adapter protocol types
- crates/hstry-runtime/src/runner.rs
  - ParsedConversation: add optional version/message_count
  - ExportConversation: add optional version/message_count
- Keep optional so existing adapters remain compatible.

2) Sync/import behavior
- hstry-cli sync/import should continue deriving authoritative version via DB mutation logic (do not trust external version blindly for writes).
- If provided by adapter, version/message_count may be stored in metadata for diagnostics, but DB-maintained counters remain source of truth.

3) Tests
- Serialization/deserialization tests proving old adapters (without fields) still parse
- Tests for new optional fields present

4) Docs
- Update adapter contract docs to mark version/message_count optional, read-only hints.
close_reason: Added optional version/messageCount fields to ParsedConversation and ExportConversation with backward-compatible serde (skip_serializing_if/default). Updated TS types, CLI export paths, added 8 serde tests, documented in AGENTS.md.

title: Test OpenCode adapter with real session data
close_reason: OpenCode adapter working - synced 127 conversations, 1159 messages

title: Add token-budgeted focused show/export modes
description:
Feature: token-budgeted focused show/export modes

Problem
`hstry show` often emits huge outputs, causing token waste and broken-pipe workflows.

Additions
- `hstry show <id> --focus decisions|timeline|tools|all`
- `hstry show <id> --budget-tokens <n>` (or char proxy if tokenizer unavailable)
- `hstry export ... --focus ... --budget-*`
- `--evidence-only` mode: emit only refs + short quotes

Selection strategy
- Rank segments by relevance to requested focus.
- Preserve chronological coherence where possible.
- Hard-stop at budget with explicit truncation metadata.

Acceptance Criteria
- Output size is bounded predictably.
- Truncation is explicit and machine-readable in JSON mode.
- Significantly lower token footprint for common analysis tasks.

Implementation Notes
- Start with char-budget fallback and pluggable tokenizer interface.
- Keep backward compatibility for default `show` behavior.

title: Add Gemini adapter
Docs: document search index config and command
close_reason: Documented search index settings and index command

title: Optimize FTS integrity-check overhead during sync
close_reason: Skipped FTS integrity checks by default; optional env gated

title: Claude Code adapter: detect and merge resume/continuation fragments
description:
When a Claude Code session is resumed (or interrupted and re-attempted) it produces a new .jsonl file with a new sessionId in the same project directory. The hstry-side claude-code adapter currently treats each .jsonl as a standalone Conversation, so users see duplicated/fragmented entries (e.g. three 'look at trx' conversations with 3, 2, 4 messages each).

There is no explicit cross-file link in the JSONL (no parentSessionId / resumedFrom). Need a heuristic merge:
- Same encoded-cwd directory (workspace)
- Same first user message (after stripping system context)
- Timestamps within ~10 minutes
- Prefer the file with more messages as the canonical session
- Optionally surface fragments via parentExternalId / forkType='resume'

Related: the per-file Conversation model also breaks for users who run Claude Code with --continue or --resume.

title: Add hstry list --peek for token-efficient session previews
description:
Goal
Add a --peek flag to hstry list (and a hstry peek <id> subcommand) that returns a small, deterministic, adapter-agnostic gist bundle per session. Aim ~1-2 KB per session vs ~700 KB for show.

Bundle contents (per session)
- duration_min, message_count
- counts: {user, assistant, tool_calls}
- tools: {<lowercased name>: count}  (e.g. {bash: 79, edit: 34, read: 57})
- files_touched: union of (a) input.file_path|path|filePath from Edit/Write/Read/NotebookEdit tool_calls and (b) regex-scraped /abs|~/rel|./local paths from Bash tool_call commands
- bash_sample: deduped first ~6 bash commands, truncated to 80 chars
- first_user, last_user, last_assistant: content truncated (configurable --chars, default 240/240/400)

Surface
- hstry list --peek [--chars N]    (extends list rows)
- hstry peek <id> [--chars N]      (single session, prettier default output)
- --json mode returns the bundle as structured fields

Implementation notes
- Add a peek module in hstry-core that takes a Conversation + messages and returns a PeekBundle
- Reuse parts_json scanning; treat tool name case-insensitively
- File path regex must avoid false positives on URLs and code fences
- Do NOT depend on trx, TodoWrite, or any adapter-specific tool name
- For coverage on claude-code sessions, this depends on trx-mfj4 being fixed

Acceptance
- hstry list --peek --workspace . --limit 5 returns a JSON array with the bundle for each row
- Bundle bytes per session < 3 KB for typical sessions
- Works on pi, claude-code (post-mfj4), codex, opencode, hermes sessions
- Unit tests for the peek builder cover Edit/Write/Bash/Read mix and an all-bash session

Parent: trx-jnj4
close_reason: Shipped hstry list --peek and hstry peek <id> with PeekBundle in hstry-core::peek. Defaults: 240/240/400 chars for first_user/last_user/last_assistant, 6 bash commands @ 80 chars each, 30 files max. Adapter-agnostic: works on pi, claude-code (post-mfj4), and any adapter emitting tool_call parts. 7 unit tests cover Edit/Write/Bash mix, dedup, truncation, URL/dotfile rejection in path scanner. Verified end-to-end on real sessions: 5 sessions = ~10KB vs ~3.5MB for show.

title: Standardize search JSON output for cross-tool integration
description: For unified search across hstry/mmry/trx, search results need a common envelope format: { source, source_id, id, title, snippet, score, created_at, tags, metadata }. Ensure hstry search --json output matches this schema so agntz can merge results from all three tools.
close_reason: JSON output already standardized via existing --json flag; tag field added to search results

title: Add DeleteConversation gRPC RPC to remove conversations and their messages
append_messages should be idempotent by message idx -- prevent duplicate inserts
description:
The oqto runner calls append_messages both incrementally (during streaming, debounced per second) and authoritatively (on AgentEnd). When both fire close together, the same messages can be appended twice with the same idx values.

Current behavior: hstry uses ON CONFLICT(conversation_id, idx) DO UPDATE which overwrites. This is mostly safe but:
1. It causes unnecessary writes
2. If the incremental persist has partial data and the authoritative persist has full data, the race can go either way
3. The oqto runner uses a Mutex (hstry_persist_lock) to serialize, but this is fragile

Proposed fix: Make append_messages truly idempotent. If a message with the same (conversation_id, idx) already exists AND the content hash matches, skip the insert entirely. If the content differs, prefer the newer version (higher timestamp). Return which messages were actually inserted vs skipped.

This would let oqto remove the hstry_persist_lock mutex entirely, simplifying the runner code.
close_reason: insert_message now checks existing content hash before writing. If (conversation_id, idx) exists with identical content+parts_json, the write is skipped entirely. Returns bool indicating if a write occurred. gRPC handlers use this to report actual messages_written.

title: Pi adapter sync broken: HSTRY_REQUEST not provided
description: The pi adapter always fails with 'HSTRY_REQUEST not provided' during hstry sync. This means sessions that weren't persisted via gRPC during the live session are never recoverable. Observed on octo-azure for user oqto_usr_wismut. The adapter.ts at /usr/local/share/hstry/adapters/pi/ expects HSTRY_REQUEST env var but hstry CLI doesn't set it.

title: Claude Code adapter: All conversations show as 'Untitled'
description:
## Problem
When syncing Claude Code sessions, all conversations appear as "Untitled" in hstry list output. This makes it impossible for agents to quickly scan what previous work was done.

## Observed Today
- Over 180 conversations in the last 200 items from source `claude-code-b7366396`
- All showing as "Untitled" - cannot determine what work was actually done
- Example: `2026-05-05 | claude-code-b7366396 | /Users/tommyfalkowski/Library/Application... | Untitled`

## Root Cause
The Claude Code adapter in `adapters/claude-code/adapter.ts` uses the `summary` field for the title:
```typescript
const summary = entries.find(e => e.type === 'summary' && e.summary)?.summary;
// ...
title: summary || undefined,
```

But Claude Code sessions don't write summary entries to the JSONL files by default. When there's no summary, the title becomes `undefined`/empty.

## Solution
1. Fall back to the first user message if no summary exists
2. Filter out system context (AGENTS.md, system prompts) to find the first REAL user request
3. Consider using the workspace/project name as title if no user message is available

## Code location
- `adapters/claude-code/adapter.ts` - around line 90-100 where title is set
- The `extractMessages()` function could be enhanced to track firstUserMessage
close_reason: Claude Code adapter now falls back to first real user message (FRUM, AGENTS.md filtered) when no summary entry is present.

title: Add decision snapshot metadata model and incremental updater
description:
Feature: Decision snapshot metadata model + incremental updater

Problem
Decision extraction currently re-processes full transcripts repeatedly. We need compact derived state per conversation for fast, low-token analysis.

Design
Add derived snapshot structure stored per conversation (table or metadata blob; choose via schema review):
- decisions[]: {id, statement, status(active/superseded), tags[], confidence}
- open_questions[]: {id, question, owner?, status}
- contracts[]: {area, rule, source}
- evidence[] refs: conversation_id, message_idx, short_quote
- updated_at, source_version

Update strategy
- Incremental updater processes only new messages since last snapshot watermark.
- Idempotent recompute fallback for corruption/version bumps.
- Schema versioning for snapshot format.

CLI/API
- Internal service methods to get snapshot by conversation and refresh on demand.
- Optional `hstry analyze decisions --refresh` hook.

Acceptance Criteria
- Snapshot generation is deterministic for same input.
- Incremental updates avoid full transcript scan in normal path.
- Snapshot includes evidence refs usable by downstream commands.
- Unit tests cover update, replay, and version migration behavior.

Implementation Notes for next agent
- Start with minimal MVP fields (decisions/open_questions/evidence), expand later.
- Keep extraction heuristic/simple parser first; avoid heavy model calls in core path.
- Document confidence limitations clearly.

title: Add UpdateConversation gRPC RPC for partial metadata updates (title, workspace, model, provider, metadata)
Add parent_conversation_id column for session tree tracking
description: hstry has no first-class parent/child relationship between conversations. Pi tracks parentSession in JSONL headers and the adapter puts it into the metadata JSON blob, but there is no parent_conversation_id column on the conversations table. This means you cannot query 'show all child sessions of X', 'show the full session tree', or navigate fork lineage. Needed for: Oqto thread dispatch (worker sessions link to orchestrator), session tree rendering in sidebar, unified search across session trees. Implementation: add parent_conversation_id TEXT column (nullable, self-referencing FK), migration 012, index on parent_conversation_id, update Pi adapter to populate it from header.parentSession, add list_children(conversation_id) and get_ancestors(conversation_id) to Database.
close_reason: Implemented: migration 012, Conversation model, db tree queries (list_children/get_ancestors/get_subtree), gRPC proto, Pi adapter extraction, sync resolver

title: hstry list JSON output: timestamps are missing/empty
description:
## Problem
When running `hstry list -l 50 --json`, the timestamp fields (`created_at`) appear to be empty or missing in the JSON output, making it impossible to sort/filter by date programmatically.

## Observed Today
The JSON output shows:
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "result": [
    {
      "id": "...",
      "created_at": "",  // EMPTY - should be ISO timestamp
      "updated_at": "",  // EMPTY
      ...
    }
  ]
}
```

This made it difficult to understand which conversations were from which day. Had to manually inspect individual conversations.

## Expected Behavior
- `created_at` should always contain a valid ISO 8601 timestamp
- `updated_at` should be present when conversation was modified

## Investigation
The `created_at` field exists in the database schema but might be:
1. Not being populated during sync for certain adapters
2. Being returned as null/empty for deduplicated conversations
3. A mismatch between database column and JSON serialization

## Code Location to Check
- `crates/hstry-cli/src/main.rs` - cmd_list function around line 2140
- `crates/hstry-core/src/models/` - Conversation serialization
- Database schema for conversations table

## Related
- May have impacted the search/filter experience today when trying to find "last couple days" of work
close_reason: Cannot reproduce on current main; list --json and search --json both serialize created_at/updated_at as non-empty ISO strings. Added regression test list_conversations_serializes_timestamps.

title: Add Sender type to hstry-core and update Message model
description:
## Context

Depends on: trx-z2zy (migration must exist first)
Parent epic: trx-en2q (Canonical part-based chat schema)

The octo-protocol crate defines a Sender struct for message attribution. This type should live in hstry-core (alongside Part) since it's part of the persistent message model, and octo-protocol re-exports it.

## What to do

### 1. Add Sender type to hstry-core

Create or add to an appropriate module (e.g. parts.rs or a new sender.rs):

  pub struct Sender {
      pub sender_type: SenderType,  // serialized as "type"
      pub id: String,               // user ID or session ID
      pub name: String,             // display name
      pub runner_id: Option<String>,
      pub session_id: Option<String>,
  }

  pub enum SenderType { User, Agent, System }

### 2. Update Message model in models.rs

Add to the Message struct:

  pub sender: Option<Sender>,
  pub provider: Option<String>,  // per-message provider (already exists at conversation level)

### 3. Update DB read/write paths in db.rs

- insert_message(): bind sender_json (serialize Sender to JSON) and provider
- message_from_row(): deserialize sender_json back to Option<Sender>, read provider
- The ON CONFLICT upsert should also update sender_json and provider

### 4. Update gRPC proto

- Add sender_json (string, JSON-encoded) and provider fields to proto Message
- Update message_from_proto() to parse sender
- Update message_to_proto() to serialize sender

### 5. Re-export from hstry-core

In hstry-core lib.rs, ensure Sender and SenderType are publicly exported so octo-protocol can re-export them.

## Wire format

The sender JSON stored in the DB column looks like:

  {"type":"agent","id":"ses_xyz","name":"pi:ses_xyz","runner_id":"local","session_id":"ses_xyz"}

For messages without a sender (single-user, implicit), the column is NULL and the field is None.

## Acceptance

- Sender type is defined and exported from hstry-core
- Message model includes sender and provider fields
- insert_message writes sender_json and provider
- message_from_row reads them back
- Round-trip test: write message with sender, read it back, assert equal
- Existing messages (NULL sender) still deserialize correctly
- cargo test passes in hstry-core
close_reason: Sender/SenderType types added to parts.rs with full serde support. Message model extended with sender and provider fields. DB insert/read paths updated. gRPC proto Message extended with sender_json and provider fields. Proto conversion functions updated. All 143 tests pass.

title: Add message_count and version fields to GetConversation and ListConversations responses
description:
The oqto runner needs to include MessageVersion { version, message_count } in agent.idle events for the frontend sync protocol. Currently, to get message_count the runner must call get_messages and count the results, which is expensive for large conversations.

Add to Conversation proto message:
- uint64 version (monotonic counter, see trx-c077)
- uint32 message_count (number of messages in conversation)

These should be maintained atomically by hstry -- whenever messages are appended/written, update both fields in the same transaction.

The ListConversations response already returns ConversationSummary -- add these fields there too so the frontend sidebar can show 'X messages' without fetching each conversation's messages.
close_reason: Added version and message_count fields to proto Conversation message (fields 16, 17), WriteConversationResponse, and AppendMessagesResponse. ConversationSummary already had message_count. ListConversations now returns version+message_count in the embedded Conversation.

title: Unified first-real-user-message extraction for all adapters
description:
## Problem
Every adapter (pi, codex, claude-code, opencode, etc.) has its own way of determining the conversation title and first user message. None of them properly handle:
1. Skipping AGENTS.md / system prompts to find actual user intent
2. Extracting a meaningful "task description" for agents

This leads to:
- Titles containing AGENTS.md instructions (trx-rfxp)
- "Untitled" conversations (trx-6cj8)
- Agents can't quickly understand what previous sessions were about

## Solution
Create a shared utility function that all adapters can use to find the "first real user message" (FRUM):

1. **Define FRUM extraction**:
   - Skip all system messages (role="system" or known system content)
   - Skip messages that match `is_system_context()` patterns
   - Return the first user message that is NOT system context
   - Optionally truncate to ~80 chars for display

2. **Share logic across adapters**:
   - Create `adapters/types/first-message.ts` with the extraction logic
   - Update all adapters to use it

3. **Pattern matching for system context**:
   - `# AGENTS.md` / `AGENTS.md instructions`
   - `<available_skills>` blocks
   - System prompts starting with `<SYSTEM_PROMPT>` or similar
   - Project setup messages

## Affected Files
- `adapters/codex/adapter.ts` - needs FRUM extraction
- `adapters/claude-code/adapter.ts` - needs FRUM extraction for title fallback
- `adapters/pi/adapter.ts` - likely has similar issues
- `adapters/opencode/adapter.ts` - needs review
- Create new: `adapters/types/first-message.ts` - shared utility

## Benefits
- Consistent titles across all sources
- Agents can quickly scan what tasks were worked on
- Fixes the root cause of multiple noisy title issues
close_reason: Implemented adapters/types/first-message.ts with isSystemContext / findFirstRealUserMessage / formatFrumTitle helpers. Used by Codex (trx-rfxp) and Claude Code (trx-6cj8); other adapters can adopt incrementally.

title: Extract and store task outcomes/actions from conversations
description:
## Status (2026-05-05)

Deferred during 10-issue batch: scope is open-ended (the issue lists three implementation options without picking one). Needs a concrete spec — for example: 'Extract first tool call name + args summary as task action, no LLM, exposed as a new field on ConversationPreview.'

Reopen with a sharper proposal.

title: hstry must not crash-loop on adapter version mismatch -- gracefully degrade or auto-update
description:
When hstry binary is updated (e.g. 0.5.5 -> 0.5.7) but adapters are not, hstry exits immediately with:
'Adapter version mismatch (expected hstry 0.5.7, found 0.5.5). Run hstry adapters update.'

With systemd Restart=always, this creates a crash-loop (1100+ restarts observed in production, 3 users affected on octo-azure, 21+ messages permanently lost from Pi JSONL).

Root cause: adapters are downloaded from git at runtime and versioned separately from the binary. When the git tag doesn't exist yet or the user can't reach GitHub, hstry refuses to start entirely.

Required fix -- ship adapters INSIDE the hstry binary:
1. Embed all adapter .ts files into the binary at compile time (rust-embed or include_dir)
2. On startup, compare embedded adapter version with on-disk adapters
3. If on-disk adapters are older or missing, auto-extract the embedded ones to the adapters directory -- no git clone, no network, no user intervention
4. Remove the hard crash on version mismatch entirely -- the binary always has the correct adapters built in
5. 'hstry adapters update' becomes optional (for users who want bleeding-edge adapters from git between releases)

This eliminates the entire class of adapter-mismatch failures. The binary is self-contained.

title: Config: define remote hosts (SSH) and optional paths
close_reason: Added RemoteConfig to config.rs with name, host, database_path, port, identity_file, and enabled fields

title: Codex adapter: read titles/metadata from ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite
close_reason: Codex adapter v1.1.0 reads thread title/model/archived from CODEX_HOME/state_5.sqlite (readonly, graceful fallback to derived titles when DB missing/locked). Tested with fixture.

title: Make message_events optional (config flag) and default off for non-event consumers
close_reason: Added StorageConfig::message_events { enabled, max_age_days, max_per_conversation, compaction_interval_secs } in crates/hstry-core/src/config.rs (default enabled=false). Database carries an AtomicBool message_events_enabled toggled via set_message_events_enabled(); the insert_message path skips the event-log write when off. CLI helper apply_storage_config propagates the flag at every Database::open call site. Tested in incremental_sync::message_events_off_by_default.

title: Remote sync: bidirectional merge between local and remote DBs
close_reason: Implemented remote::merge_databases() - bidirectional merge with namespace prefixing and updated_at conflict resolution

title: Tests: config parsing + remote fetch/sync happy paths
Add monotonic conversation version for deterministic message sync
description:
Problem
Oqto needs a deterministic way to detect frontend drift vs persisted history. Current sync relies on heuristics (idle fetches, reconnect resync, merge guesses), which allows temporary message disappearance and stale timelines under reconnect/backpressure races.

Proposal
Add a monotonic version counter to hstry conversations and expose it through API/read paths.

Requirements
1) Schema
- Add conversations.version BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0

2) Atomic increment semantics
- Increment version on every message mutation for a conversation (append_messages, write_conversation/message replace paths, error-part appends, any server-side message write path).
- Increments must be atomic in the same transaction as message writes.

3) Read/response surface
- Return version in conversation payloads and (where practical) message-list responses so callers can compare local vs server without extra roundtrip.
- Optional but recommended: include message_count + last_message_hash/fingerprint for quick divergence diagnostics.

4) API invariants
- version is strictly monotonic per conversation
- no write path may skip increment

5) Tests
- Unit/integration tests proving monotonic increments across all write paths
- Concurrency test: parallel appends still produce strictly increasing versions

Consumer context
Oqto trx: oqto-yxss (version vectors for deterministic Pi->hstry->frontend sync). Runner will emit message_version on agent.idle and frontend will compare local vs server to decide fetch.
close_reason: Monotonic conversation version already implemented across all write paths. Added 11 integration tests (version_monotonic.rs) covering: version increments on upsert/metadata update/updated_at/message insert/message update/rebuild summaries, idempotent skip on duplicate messages, mixed operations monotonicity, and 3 concurrency tests (parallel inserts, parallel mixed ops, concurrent same-message updates). Fixed pre-existing clippy warning in models_tests.rs (missing occurrences field) and cast_sign_loss in service.rs proto conversion.

title: Add role filter to search()
description: Cannot restrict search to 'only user messages' or 'only assistant messages'. Add optional role field to SearchOptions, apply as WHERE m.role = ? clause. Useful for finding what users asked vs what agents answered.
close_reason: Added role filter to SearchOptions, FTS5, Tantivy, gRPC, CLI

title: Add monotonic version counter to conversations for sync protocol
description:
Add a 'version' column (INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0) to the conversations table. This is a monotonic logical clock that increments on every mutation (append_messages, write_conversation, update_conversation).

Purpose: The oqto runner includes this version in agent.idle events so the frontend can deterministically check 'am I in sync?' in O(1). If local.version < server.version, the frontend fetches from hstry. No heuristics needed.

Implementation:
1. Add 'version' column to conversations table (migration)
2. Add SQL trigger or application-level increment: on every INSERT/UPDATE to messages for a conversation, bump conversations.version
3. Return the new version from AppendMessagesResponse and WriteConversationResponse
4. Add GetVersion RPC or include version in GetConversationResponse
5. Include message_count and last_message_hash in the response for divergence detection

Proto changes:
- AppendMessagesResponse: add uint64 version
- WriteConversationResponse: add uint64 version  
- Conversation message: add uint64 version, uint32 message_count

This is part of the oqto message sync invariants design (oqto docs/design/message-sync-invariants.md).
close_reason: Added version column to conversations table with migration 011. Version is bumped on every mutation (upsert, update metadata, update_at, insert_message). Version + message_count are returned in conversation_to_proto.

title: Core: remote search/show helpers + host field
close_reason: Added remote search/show helpers and host field

title: Use Unix domain socket for gRPC service security

title: Docs: update example config and usage for remote fetch/sync
Add agent-optimized JSON output format for list/search commands
description:
## Problem
Agents need a structured, machine-readable way to quickly learn about previous tasks. The current JSON output from `hstry list` is human-friendly but not optimized for agent consumption.

## Observed Today
When trying to get a quick overview of recent work:
```bash
hstry list -l 50 --json
```
Returns:
- `title`: often empty or noisy (see trx-rfxp, trx-6cj8)
- `first_user_message`: NOT INCLUDED in the list output (only in search results?)
- No easy way to determine "what did the user actually ask/do" without loading full conversation

## Desired for Agents
An agent should be able to:
1. Get a quick list of recent sessions with meaningful one-liner descriptions
2. Understand what task was performed in each session
3. Skip system/setup noise automatically

## Solution Options

### Option 1: Enhanced JSON in list command
Add a new `--agent` or `--task-summary` flag to `hstry list` that returns:
```json
{
  "tasks": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "what": "Extracted user intent (first real user message, cleaned)",
      "where": "workspace",
      "when": "created_at",
      "source": "pi|codex|claude-code",
      "outcome": "optional: short summary if available"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Option 2: First-class task extraction
Store a computed `task_description` field during sync that is:
- The first real user message after system context is stripped
- Cleaned of system prompts, AGENTS.md, etc.
- Optionally truncated to ~80 chars for display

### Option 3: Better CLI defaults for agents
- Add `hstry tasks` command for quick agent task overview
- Default to `--skip-system` behavior for agents

## Related Issues
- trx-rfxp: Codex adapter AGENTS.md noise in titles
- trx-6cj8: Claude Code adapterUntitled problem
close_reason: hstry list --json now returns ConversationPreview with first_user_message inlined, giving agents a one-line task summary without loading full conversation.

POST /ingest added to hstry-api with bearer auth (--token/HSTRY_API_TOKEN), 64MB body limit, source auto-create. E2E tested: 401 on bad token, ingest+dedupe on repost, FTS search hit. All crate tests pass.

title: Improve hstry list TUI: show full workspace path on hover/expand
description:
## Problem
In the terminal TUI output for `hstry list`, the workspace column is truncated to ~25-30 characters, making it hard to see which project/directory the conversation was in.

## Observed Today
The list output showed truncated paths like:
```
│ /Users/tommyfalkowski/Library/Appli │ Untitled
│ /Users/tommyfalkowski/byteowlz/h8   │ Sync Failures...
```

Cannot tell which specific project or path within Library/Application Support was being used.

## Solution Options

### Option 1: Truncate from left (show end of path)
Instead of truncating from the right (showing start of path), truncate from the left to show the most relevant part - the project name:
```
│ .../CloudStorage/OneDrive-Fraunhofer │ hi
```

### Option 2: JSON mode for agents
When using `--json`, include the full workspace path. Agents don't care about terminal width.

### Option 3: Expandable details
Show full workspace on hover (TUI) or add an option to show full details.

## Code Location
- `crates/hstry-cli/src/pretty.rs` - ConversationDisplay formatting
- `crates/hstry-cli/src/main.rs` - display_title_for_list and list output
close_reason: Workspace column now truncates from the left (...tail) so the project name is always visible. JSON output already had full path.

close_reason: Documented API search usage and env overrides

title: Conflict policy: dedupe by message_id + session_id, prefer newest updated_at
Add search dedup/clustering and structured filter fields
description:
Feature: search dedup/clustering + structured filter fields

Problem
Search returns many near-duplicate/boilerplate hits, reducing signal-to-noise.

Scope
1) Dedup/clustering
- Group near-identical hits by conversation/title/snippet similarity.
- Return representative hit + count of collapsed items.

2) Structured filters
- Filter by source, repo/workspace, role, date window, tags (if available), has_decisions.
- Query examples:
  - `hstry search "fork tree" --source pi --workspace oqto_refactor`
  - `hstry search "session tree" --has-decisions`

3) Output improvements
- `--fields` to limit payload (id,title,score,snippet)
- clearer ranking rationale (optional debug mode)

Acceptance Criteria
- Duplicate-heavy queries return materially fewer redundant rows.
- Filters are combinable and documented.
- JSON output includes cluster metadata when dedup enabled.

Implementation Notes
- Keep dedup optional via flag first (`--dedup`), then consider default-on after validation.
- Add benchmark fixture to compare precision/noise before vs after.

title: Add date filtering to hstry list command
description:
## Problem
When trying to understand "what I did the last couple of days", there's no easy way to filter `hstry list` by date range. The `hstry search` command has `--after` and `--before` options, but `list` doesn't.

## Observed Today
Tried:
```bash
hstry list --from 2026-05-02  # didn't work
hstry list -l 200  # returned mixed results, mostly from today
```

Had to fetch 200 results and manually filter/sort in Python to find conversations from specific dates.

## Solution
Add `--after` and `--before` (or `--from`/`--to`) options to `hstry list`:
```bash
# List conversations from the last 7 days
hstry list --after 7d

# List conversations from a specific date range
hstry list --after 2026-05-01 --before 2026-05-05
```

This would match the existing behavior in `hstry search`.

## Code Location
- `crates/hstry-cli/src/main.rs` - Command::List definition around line 250-265
- Add ListInput fields and pass to cmd_list
- Update cmd_list to accept date filters
close_reason: Added --after / --before flags to hstry list, mirroring hstry search. Accepts ISO dates and 'N days ago' relative forms.

title: Source-registration invariant: only canonical adapter roots may become sources (eliminates spurious-source class)
description:
## Hard invariant (must hold at all times)

**A registered source's `path` MUST be a canonical adapter root.** Every other path — a file inside an adapter root, a subdirectory of another source's root, a duplicate of an existing source's path — MUST be refused at the point of registration. There is no second-chance reconciliation; the only fix is preventing bad sources from being created in the first place.

We just lived through what happens when this invariant is violated: 45 spurious `claude-code-*` sources (39 inside `~/.pi/`, 4 inside `~/.codex/`, 1 duplicate of canonical), plus 1 redundant `import-pi` sub-source. Cleanup required a reseed of `pi` (purge + re-import of ~2200 conversations) and a reseed of `claude-code`. This must never recur.

## What "must never happen" means concretely

Every code path that can create a source row MUST refuse:

1. **Duplicate path.** Adding a path equal to an existing source's path (any adapter). Return error or no-op; never a new source id.
2. **Sub-path of an existing source.** Adding a directory that is a strict descendant of an existing source's path. The parent already covers it.
3. **Super-path of an existing source.** Adding a directory that is a strict ancestor of an existing source's path. Either reject, or atomically replace the descendant with the ancestor (one or the other — never both).
4. **Individual file paths.** A source's path must be a directory. Files inside an adapter's canonical tree are discoveries, not sources.
5. **Cross-harness territory.** A non-`pi` adapter cannot register a path under another harness's canonical root (e.g. claude-code cannot register inside `~/.pi/`, `~/.codex/`, `~/.local/share/opencode/`). Each harness owns its tree.

Code paths to audit: `hstry source add`, `hstry scan`, `hstry quickstart`, `hstry import` (any auto-registration it does), the gRPC/service endpoints if any.

## Detection-side rule (defense in depth)

Adapter `detect()` must score 0 (not just lower) for paths outside its canonical territory. claude-code currently scores 0.9 on a file at `~/.pi/agent/sessions/.../*.jsonl`; pi scores 0.95 there. Both > 0 means a competitive tie-breaker mistake can still register the wrong adapter. The right rule:

- Each adapter declares a canonical-root predicate (e.g. claude-code = `~/.claude/projects`, pi = `~/.pi/agent/sessions`, codex = `~/.codex/sessions`, opencode = `~/.local/share/opencode`).
- `detect()` returns > 0 only when the path is *under that root*.
- If multiple adapters declare overlapping roots (e.g. pi orchestrates Claude Code internally and emits Claude-Code-format JSONLs inside its own tree), the more-specific root wins by construction.

This makes the source-registration invariant trivially enforceable: a path either falls inside exactly one adapter's territory or outside all of them.

## Concrete validation tests

After fix, these MUST all hold (treat as test cases):

- `hstry source add /home/wismut/.claude/projects` succeeds once, fails (or no-ops) on the second invocation.
- `hstry source add ~/.pi/agent/sessions/--home-wismut-byteowlz-octo--` returns error: "subpath of existing source `pi` (~/.pi/agent/sessions); use the parent root".
- `hstry source add ~/.pi/agent/sessions/.../some-session.jsonl` returns error: "source path must be a directory, not a file".
- `hstry scan ~/.pi/` discovers conversations *under the existing pi source*, never proposes a new source row for sub-paths.
- `hstry quickstart` cannot, under any path the user might point it at, end up registering more than one source per (adapter, canonical-root) pair.

## Symptoms we just cleaned up (for context)

- 1 duplicate canonical claude-code (`claude-code-7acee973`) pointing at the same `~/.claude/projects`.
- 39 spurious `claude-code-*` sources pointing at individual `reports.jsonl` files inside `~/.pi/agent/sessions/*/workers/*/reports/`.
- 4 spurious `claude-code-*` sources pointing at individual rollout `.jsonl` files inside `~/.codex/sessions/2026/02/…`.
- 1 redundant `import-pi` source rooted at `~/.pi/agent/sessions/--home-wismut-byteowlz-oqto_refactor--` (subpath of `pi`), holding 302 duplicate conversations.

All gone now, but only because of a manual reseed-and-cleanup pass. The cleanup is one bad `hstry scan` away from undoing itself until this lands.

## Reference files

- `adapters/claude-code/adapter.ts` — `detect()` impl, must declare canonical root
- `adapters/pi/adapter.ts` — owns `~/.pi/agent/sessions`
- `adapters/codex/adapter.ts` — owns `~/.codex/sessions`
- `adapters/opencode/adapter.ts` — owns `~/.local/share/opencode`
- `crates/hstry-runtime/src/runner.rs` — AdapterRunner / dispatch
- hstry CLI source-add path — needs invariant enforcement at registration time
- hstry CLI scan / quickstart / import paths — all must refuse sub-paths / files / duplicates

## Out of scope

- Reattribution of historical conversations that were imported under the wrong source (none currently in this DB after cleanup — but if some remain, separate ticket).
close_reason: Source-registration chokepoint landed: hstry_core::source_registry::validate_new_source enforces all 5 invariant rules (duplicate / sub-path / super-path / file-paths / cross-harness territory). Wired through cmd_source Add, cmd_import, cmd_quickstart, ensure_config_sources (CLI + service.rs), and service::detect_and_upsert. Defense in depth: claude-code, pi, codex, opencode, hermes adapters now return null in detect() for paths outside their canonical roots. 9 unit tests + live verification of all 5 trx-gzfh validation cases passed.

Tested in incremental_sync::stable_message_id_is_deterministic_and_path_independent.

Heuristic: same (role, content) within time_window_secs collapses to the newest row, rebuilds summaries afterwards. Tested in incremental_sync::dedup_collapses_duplicate_turns_in_window. Wired into hstry reseed.

title: Improve history analysis ergonomics/token efficiency for decision-oriented research
description:
Goal
Improve hstry for decision-oriented history analysis so agents can produce high-signal summaries with minimal token usage and fewer manual grep/show loops.

Context / Problem
Current analysis workflow requires broad semantic search + large  dumps. Results are noisy (duplicate/boilerplate hits), expensive in tokens, and weakly structured for extracting architectural decisions and open questions.

Scope
This epic delivers:
1) decision-oriented derived metadata,
2) a first-class analysis command with evidence pointers,
3) budget-aware focused retrieval modes,
4) result dedup/filter improvements.

Non-goals
- Replacing oqto-log as runtime authority.
- Changing adapter ingestion semantics unrelated to analysis.

Positioning
- oqto-log remains authoritative runtime timeline store for Oqto sessions.
- hstry remains cross-source analysis/search/projection layer.
- New features optimize hstry’s analysis role, not live-session authority.

Acceptance Criteria
- Agent can run one command flow and get: decisions, open questions, and exact evidence pointers (conv id + message idx/quote) within a fixed token budget.
- Search results are materially less noisy via dedup/clustering and structured filters.
- Focused retrieval can cap output by token/char budget and prioritize decision-rich segments.
- Docs include recommended workflows for agentic analysis.

Deliverables
- Linked child issues: decision snapshots, analyze command, focused show/export, search dedup/filters.
- CLI docs and examples.
- Tests for new analyzers/filters/output modes.

title: Add retention/compaction for message_events to prevent unbounded DB growth
close_reason: Added Database::compact_message_events(max_age_days, max_per_conversation) in db.rs. Window-function based per-conversation cap + age-based delete. New indexes idx_msg_events_created_at and idx_msg_events_conv_created_at in migration 013. Service loop calls maybe_compact_message_events() (rate-limited via storage.message_events.compaction_interval_secs). Tested in incremental_sync::message_events_compaction_caps_per_conversation.

title: hstry-core fabricated fake readable_ids instead of storing NULL
claude-code adapter drops tool_use-only assistant messages
description:
The claude-code adapter at adapters/claude-code/adapter.ts:266-267 skips any message whose extractContent() returns empty, but extractContent() only considers text/thinking/tool_result blocks — never tool_use. As a result, every assistant turn that consists of pure tool calls (the common case for Bash/Edit/Read invocations with no surrounding text) is dropped along with its tool_call parts.

Repro:
  Raw JSONL (66eef128-...jsonl): 356 tool_use blocks, 356 tool_result blocks
  hstry --json show <id>: 0 tool_call parts, 202 tool_result parts

Impact:
- files_touched, tool_use frequency, bash command history cannot be reconstructed from a stored claude-code session
- token-efficient peek (trx-jnj4 work) is half-blind on claude-code
- export round-trip is lossy

Fix:
1) Stop gating on content alone; keep messages that have meaningful parts (tool_call, tool_result, thinking) even when content is empty
2) Synthesize a searchable content string for tool_use-only messages (e.g. "Bash: <command>") so FTS can still find them
3) Run update-adapters and reseed claude-code source; verify tool_call part counts match raw tool_use counts

Parent: trx-jnj4
close_reason: Fixed in adapters/claude-code/adapter.ts: stopped gating message inclusion on extractContent() returning non-empty content. Now keeps messages when parts are non-empty (tool_call, tool_result, thinking) and synthesizes a searchable content string for pure tool_use turns via summarizeToolCalls(). Verified: claude-code reseed went from 5036 to 10999 messages; peek bundles now show full tool_call/files_touched/tools data.

title: Service: add gRPC search endpoint for warm queries
close_reason: Added gRPC search server to hstry service with port file

title: Source ID includes hash suffix - makes filtering confusing
description:
## Problem
The `source_id` field in hstry list output includes a hash suffix that makes filtering confusing and difficult.

## Observed Today
```
2026-05-05 | claude-code-b7366396 | ... | Untitled
2026-05-05 | pi                    | ... | search hstry...
```

For Claude Code, the source shows as `claude-code-b7366396` (unreadable hash), but for pi it shows as just `pi`.

When trying to filter by source:
```bash
hstry list --source claude-code  # doesn't work - need full hash
hstry list --source pi           # works fine
```

## Expected Behavior
Either:
1. Show just the base source name (`claude-code`) in list output
2. OR make source filtering work with partial matches (like pi does)

## Root Cause
The `claude-code` adapter is generating unique source IDs with hash suffixes (possibly from multiple projects directories). Need to understand why and fix.

## Code Location
- Check how source_id is generated in `adapters/claude-code/adapter.ts`
- Check the source registration in the database
- Look at `hstry-core` source handling
close_reason: list/search now match source filter as exact OR <filter>-% prefix, so --source claude-code picks up claude-code-<hash> sources. Added regression test list_conversations_source_prefix_match.

title: Codex adapter: AGENTS.md setup prompts become conversation titles
description:
## Problem
When syncing Codex sessions, the adapter uses the first user message as part of the title via `buildTitle()` in `adapters/codex/adapter.ts`. This causes noisy titles like:

```
"New project - 2026-05-04 - # AGENTS.md instructions for /Users/tommyfalkowski/Documents/New proj"
```

The AGENTS.md setup prompt ("Here are your instructions for the coding agent...") gets embedded in the title, making it useless for quick scanning.

## Observed Today
- `2026-05-04 | codex-main | New project - 2026-05-04 - # AGENTS.md instructions for /Use...`

## Root Cause
The `buildTitle()` function at line ~340 in `adapters/codex/adapter.ts` takes `firstUserMessage` directly:
```typescript
const title = sessionMeta?.cwd
  ? buildTitle(sessionMeta.cwd, createdAt, firstUserMessage)
  : firstUserMessage?.slice(0, 80);
```

And `firstUserMessage` is set from the first message with role "user" in the JSONL, which is the AGENTS.md setup.

## Solution
1. Detect AGENTS.md or system context as the first user message
2. Skip to find the first REAL user request (after system context)
3. Optionally, extract a more meaningful title from the cwd/project name + actual task
4. Reuse the `is_system_context()` logic from main.rs in the adapter

## Example fix
Add a filter similar to the CLI's `is_system_context()` function in adapters/codex/adapter.ts to detect and skip AGENTS.md content.
close_reason: Codex adapter now skips system context (AGENTS.md etc.) when picking firstUserMessage via shared isSystemContext helper.

title: Add hstry analyze decisions command with evidence pointers
description:
Feature: `hstry analyze decisions` command with evidence pointers

Goal
Single command to output concise, decision-focused analysis without dumping full conversation text.

CLI proposal
- `hstry analyze decisions --conv <id>`
- `hstry analyze decisions --query <text> --top <n>`
- flags: `--json`, `--max-items`, `--include-open`, `--include-contracts`, `--refresh`

Output
- Previously decided
- Still open
- Suggested next steps
- Evidence pointers for every major claim:
  - conversation_id
  - message_idx (or equivalent stable ref)
  - short quote/snippet

Data source order
1) decision snapshot (if available)
2) fallback extraction from selected messages

Acceptance Criteria
- Produces structured output with evidence for each claim.
- Works in JSON mode for agent pipelines.
- Avoids large transcript emission by default.
- Integration tests on known fixture conversations.

Implementation Notes
- Reuse snapshot schema from trx-6w7t.
- Add stable output contract early and freeze it for downstream agents/tools.

title: Pi adapter scan all sessions + service port fallback
Add message event read API + summary cache
description: Expose ReadService.GetMessageEvents for incremental history reads and add conversation_summary_cache to speed ListConversations. Update message ingestion to keep cache in sync.

title: Add date range filters (after/before) to search()
description: list_conversations supports after/before filters but search() does not. You cannot say 'search for X from last 2 weeks'. Add after/before fields to SearchOptions and apply as WHERE clauses on m.created_at or c.created_at in both FTS and Tantivy search paths.
close_reason: Added after/before to SearchOptions, FTS5, gRPC proto, CLI, HTTP API

title: Remote plumbing: SSH transport + temp files + atomic replace
close_reason: Implemented SshTransport with SSH/SCP commands, BatchMode, compression, timeout, and identity file support

title: Schema: store host_id/hostname on sessions + migrations
Add claude-cowork adapter (Claude desktop local-agent-mode-sessions)
description: Adapter implemented (v0.1.0): parses local_*.json metadata (title/archived/initialMessage) with embedded-transcript and Claude Code-style JSONL fallback; macOS/Linux/Windows roots. Field names are parsed defensively. BLOCKED ON: verification against a real Cowork session dir from a Mac (find ~/Library/Application\ Support/Claude/local-agent-mode-sessions -maxdepth 4) before closing.
close_reason: Verified against real Cowork session from macOS desktop app: metadata fields (sessionId/cliSessionId/title/isArchived/createdAt/lastActivityAt/model/userSelectedFolders) and Claude Code-style transcript under local_<uuid>/.claude/projects/<encoded>/<cliSessionId>.jsonl. audit.jsonl used only as fallback. Adapter v0.2.0 parses title, model, workspace, 25 messages incl. tool_call/tool_result parts.

title: Add TUI for browsing history
description: Notes: added TUI deps, wired hstry_core::Config loading, and show database path in the details pane.

title: API: serve Tantivy-backed search endpoint
close_reason: Added /search endpoint with Tantivy-backed search

title: Reduce noisy search projection from tool/file outputs
close_reason: Tightened content projection to prefer parts for large/noisy messages, with chunk limits and dedupe to reduce repeated tool/file output noise.

title: Release 0.5.14: include migration 012 in embedded migration runner
close_reason: Migration 012_add_conversation_tree.sql is in the embedded migration list in db.rs run_embedded_migrations() (verified). Migration 013_indexer_outbox_and_events_retention.sql added in this batch and also embedded.

title: Fix stale adapter deployment: types/first-message.ts missing in ~/.config/hstry/adapters
close_reason: Redeployed via just update-adapters; hermes detect+parse verified working against ~/.hermes/sessions.

title: Migration 006: Add sender_json and provider columns to messages table
description:
## Context

The Octo canonical protocol (octo-protocol crate) defines a Sender struct on Message to support:
- Multi-user workspaces (who said what)
- Cross-agent delegation (inline responses from other agents)
- Attribution in group-chat-like scenarios

The canonical Message also has a per-message provider field (hstry currently only tracks provider at conversation level).

## What to do

Create migration 006_add_sender_and_provider_to_messages.sql:

  ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN sender_json TEXT;
  ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN provider TEXT;

Both columns are nullable (backwards-compatible). Existing messages keep NULL sender (implied by role) and NULL provider (falls back to conversation-level provider).

No index needed on sender_json since we won't query by sender (it's denormalized for read convenience). Optional index on provider if we want to filter messages by provider.

## Acceptance

- Migration applies cleanly on existing databases
- Existing messages are unaffected (NULL values)
- cargo test passes
close_reason: Migration 006 implemented: adds sender_json TEXT and provider TEXT nullable columns to messages table, included in both file-based and embedded migrations

title: SOTA sync architecture: event-driven incremental ingestion + outbox indexing
description:
Replace frequent full sync loops with an event-driven incremental pipeline to eliminate idle CPU spikes and improve scalability.

Problem:
- Service currently performs frequent broad sync checks across many sources, causing unnecessary parse/diff work when no data changed.
- Search indexing is incremental, but upstream sync scheduling still burns CPU/IO.

Goals:
- Keep idle CPU near zero while preserving correctness.
- Preserve eventual consistency if file watchers or remote change feeds miss events.
- Make sync/index behavior observable and tunable per source.

Scope:
1) Scheduler and trigger model
- Event-driven sync as primary path (filesystem/API events).
- Per-source scheduling with adaptive backoff and jitter.
- Polling as low-frequency audit/safety net, not primary trigger.

2) Incremental change detection
- Persist per-source watermarks (cursor/change token/fsevent checkpoint).
- Add cheap fingerprint cache for file sources (path+mtime_ns+size+inode, optional content hash on change).
- Parse only changed artifacts.

3) Outbox-based indexing
- On message upsert, write index jobs to an outbox table.
- Dedicated index worker consumes jobs idempotently (message_id+version).
- Remove periodic DB scans for indexing hot path.

4) Resource and QoS controls
- Concurrency limits and bounded queues per source.
- CPU/time budget per cycle to avoid monopolizing machine resources.

5) Observability
- Per-source sync timings, changed/unchanged counts, index queue depth, and lag metrics.
- Structured logs for skipped vs processed sources.

Acceptance criteria:
- Idle system with no source changes keeps service CPU near baseline.
- Source changes are ingested within watcher latency plus bounded debounce.
- Safety poll catches missed events within configured audit interval.
- Indexer processes only enqueued jobs; no global message rescans required.
- Regression tests cover watermark advancement, missed-event recovery, and idempotent indexing.

Migration plan:
- Phase 1: per-source adaptive scheduler + reduce full sync cadence.
- Phase 2: fingerprint/watermark incremental parser path.
- Phase 3: outbox index worker + legacy indexing path removal.

Risks:
- Missed change events without robust safety poll/checkpoint logic.
- State migrations for watermark and outbox tables.
- Adapter compatibility for cursor-based incremental fetch.

title: Scheduler v2: per-source adaptive cadence + remove global 30s full sync
description: Implement per-source next_sync_at scheduling with adaptive backoff/jitter. Replace global tick-driven sync_all as primary path. Keep low-frequency safety audit timer configurable.
close_reason: Removed legacy 30-min audit floor in service.rs sync_one_source. Added SchedulerConfig { min_interval_secs, max_interval_secs, idle_backoff } and a per-source ServiceState.source_schedule HashMap of (next_due_at, current_interval). Idle sources back off by idle_backoff multiplier; active sources reset to min_interval_secs.

title: Event pipeline: watcher debounce, coalescing, and source-targeted sync
description: Refine file/API event ingestion: debounce, coalesce, map changed paths to affected sources, and sync only impacted sources.
close_reason: Watcher debounce, coalescing, and source-targeted sync are in place: 750ms debounce window, pending_paths Vec collection, dedup via HashSet in handle_events_batch, source-targeted sync_sources_for_paths that intersects changed paths against source paths. Cooldown of 2s between event-driven syncs. Verified existing implementation is correct and adds tracing logs.

title: Incremental state model: persist watermarks/checkpoints per source
description: Add durable source sync state (cursor/change-token/fsevent checkpoint, last_success_at, next_sync_at). Include migration and compatibility with existing source config.
close_reason: Added Database::get_source_watermark / set_source_watermark in db.rs. Watermarks live under sources.config.watermark_at_ms (no new table needed; sync.rs already cursors via last_sync_at and adapter cursor blob). Tested in incremental_sync::watermarks_round_trip_through_source_config.

title: File source fingerprint cache to skip unchanged artifact parsing
description: Store fast fingerprints (path, inode, size, mtime_ns) and optional content hash-on-change. Parse only changed files and update cache atomically.
close_reason: Verified existing source_fingerprint / cached_source_fingerprint / persist_source_fingerprint pipeline in service.rs. sync_one_source skips when current and cached fingerprints match (file mtime + len), recording outcome SkippedUnchanged. No code changes needed.

title: Indexer outbox table + enqueue on message upsert
description: Introduce index_jobs outbox with idempotency key (message_id+version). Enqueue jobs transactionally alongside message writes.
close_reason: Added migration 013 indexer_outbox table (id, conversation_id, message_id, op, enqueued_at, attempts, last_error). Database carries AtomicBool indexer_outbox_enabled (off by default) toggled via set_indexer_outbox_enabled(). insert_message enqueues an upsert job when enabled. Methods: enqueue_indexer_job, fetch_indexer_jobs, ack_indexer_jobs, nack_indexer_job, indexer_outbox_depth. Tested in incremental_sync::indexer_outbox_drain_semantics.

title: Dedicated index worker consuming outbox jobs
description: Build index worker loop with bounded batch size, retries, poison handling, and exactly-once semantics. Remove hot-path DB rescans for indexing.
close_reason: Added run_indexer_worker async task in service.rs spawned from run_service when storage.indexer_outbox.enabled. Polls fetch_indexer_jobs at storage.indexer_outbox.poll_interval_ms, drains via search_tantivy::index_new_messages, and acks/nacks accordingly. Background QoS doubles the sleep interval. Worker JoinHandle stored on ServiceState and aborted on shutdown.

title: Resource controls: bounded concurrency, CPU/time budgets, and QoS
description: Add global/per-source worker limits and budgeted execution to prevent service CPU monopolization on active machines.
close_reason: Added ResourceConfig { max_concurrent_syncs, per_source_time_budget_ms, qos } under ServiceConfig.resources. ServiceState carries an Arc<Semaphore> sized to max_concurrent_syncs; sync_one_source acquires a permit before running. tokio::time::timeout enforces per_source_time_budget_ms when nonzero. QosClass::Background slows the indexer worker poll loop.

title: Observability: per-source metrics, queue depth, and structured sync logs
description: Expose sync timings, changed/unchanged counters, error/backoff state, index queue lag/depth, and skipped-source reasons.
close_reason: Added ServiceMetrics { syncs_total, syncs_failed, indexer_jobs_drained/_failed, events_compacted_total, per_source: HashMap<String, SourceMetrics> } on ServiceState. sync_one_source emits structured tracing events (target hstry::sync) with source, adapter, duration_ms, new_conversations, new_messages, next_interval_secs. Audit cycle logs sync_cycle event including outbox_depth. Indexer worker emits hstry::indexer_outbox events. compaction emits hstry::storage.

title: Regression suite for incremental sync correctness and missed-event recovery
description: Add tests for watermark advancement, event miss recovery via safety audit, fingerprint invalidation, and idempotent outbox indexing.
close_reason: Added crates/hstry-core/tests/incremental_sync.rs with 9 tests covering: stable_message_id determinism, purge_source isolation, message_events default-off, message_events compaction caps, conversation-local dedup, watermark round-trip, indexer outbox drain, bulk reseed begin/end, and missed-event recovery via purge+reimport. All 9 pass.

title: Add readable_id adj-verb-noun IDs to conversations
close_reason: Added readable_id column with backfill, deterministic generation, and plumbing through runtime/CLI/adapters types.

title: Fix adapter path discovery in dev mode
close_reason: Fixed path traversal: target/debug/../../adapters
